title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NodeRestriction is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NodeRestriction is set
input_row.description: Limit the Node and Pod objects that a kubelet could modify.
input_row.rationale: Using the NodeRestriction plug-in ensures that the kubelet is restricted to the Node and Pod objects that it could modify as defined. Such kubelets will only be allowed to modify their own Node API object, and only modify Pod API objects that are bound to their node. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes NodeRestriction.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure NodeRestriction plug-in on kubelets. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the -- enable-admission-plugins parameter to a value that includes NodeRestriction. --enable-admission-plugins=...,NodeRestriction,... Default Value: By default, NodeRestriction is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#noderestriction 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authorization/node/ 4. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugin_noderestriction_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to verify that the NodeRestriction admission control plugin is enabled.
- This is typically set via the --enable-admission-plugins flag in the kube-apiserver manifest (commonly found at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml in static pod setups).
- Automated checks can be performed by:
  - Using kubectl to retrieve the kube-apiserver pod specification:
    ```
    kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml
    ```
    or
    ```
    kubectl -n kube-system get pods -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.name | test("kube-apiserver")) | .spec.containers[].command'
    ```
  - Alternatively, directly reading the manifest file if accessible (for static pods).
- Parse the command line arguments for the kube-apiserver container and ensure that:
  - The --enable-admission-plugins flag is present.
  - The value for --enable-admission-plugins includes NodeRestriction (comma-separated list).
- Pass Criteria: The --enable-admission-plugins flag is set and includes NodeRestriction.
- Fail Criteria: The flag is missing, or NodeRestriction is not listed.
- Note: If --disable-admission-plugins is also set and includes NodeRestriction, this is a fail.
- Example check logic:
  - Retrieve the kube-apiserver pod spec.
  - Extract the command/args for the container.
  - Confirm NodeRestriction is present in --enable-admission-plugins and not present in --disable-admission-plugins.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.8.0 PDF
